title: Epitrachys lanceolata
intro: There is no information available for the plant Epitrachys lanceolata.
taxonomy: Epitrachys lanceolata is classified as a species within the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Epitrachys lanceolata belonging to the subfamily Apioideae. Within the subfamily Apioideae, Epitrachys lanceolata is classified as a member of the tribe Scandiceae. The tribe Scandiceae is a group of flowering plants that are primarily herbaceous and native to temperate regions. The classification of Epitrachys lanceolata provides insight into its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the family Apiaceae.
properties: Epitrachys lanceolata, also known as Lance-leaved Epitrachys, is a plant species that has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties. It has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand the therapeutic effects of Epitrachys lanceolata. The plant's leaves and other parts are traditionally used in herbal medicine, but its uses are not well-documented in Western medicine.
